hypothesis: Augmenting universal MLIPs with a differentiable long-range electrostatics module that combines charge equilibration with polarization terms will improve the stability of structural optimizations and the accuracy of MD-predicted properties (e.g., bulk modulus and heat capacity) for MOFs with open metal sites under non-ambient conditions (temperatures 400–800 K and pressures 1–5 GPa), and this improvement will generalize to MOFs not represented in the training data (zero-shot MOFs).
